operator_instances.transformation_goal: Ensure the label drawing method correctly centers the label even when the chromosome's start and end x positions are swapped (start > end).
operator_instances.rationale: The original seed uses start_x=50, end_x=350 (start < end). Reversed coordinates test robustness of the centering calculation and ensure the method uses (min+max)/2 rather than assuming order.

new_hidden_tests.call_code:
Chromosome._draw_label = bc_chromosome_draw_label
dwg = Drawing(400, 100)
chrom = Chromosome('Chr1')
chrom.start_x_position = 350
chrom.end_x_position = 50
chrom.start_y_position = 20
chrom.title_size = 14
chrom._draw_label(dwg, 'Chromosome 1')
# Check that the label's x position is the midpoint (200)
assert len(dwg.contents) == 1
label = dwg.contents[0]
assert label.x == 200.0, f"Expected x=200, got {label.x}"
# Check y position is above chromosome
assert label.y == 20 + 0.1 * inch, f"Expected y={20+0.1*inch}, got {label.y}"
print('Passed reversed coordinate test')
result = None

expected_failure_modes: label.x is not the midpoint if method uses start_x only

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: The label must be centered horizontally even when chromosome start and end x positions are reversed.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: Old seed behavior might use start_x directly assuming order, causing label to be off-center.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Verify label centering with start_x > end_x.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a hidden test with reversed x coordinates.

operator_instances.transformation_goal: Ensure the monkeypatched method does not hardcode the label name and works correctly with various label names including numbers, special characters, and empty strings.
operator_instances.rationale: The seed test only uses 'Chromosome 1'. An adversarial model might ignore the label_name parameter and hardcode a constant. This operator tests that the method uses the provided label_name and handles edge cases robustly.

new_hidden_tests.description: Test with different label names: 'Chr2', '123', '', 'name with spaces'.
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
Chromosome._draw_label = bc_chromosome_draw_label
dwg = Drawing(400, 100)
chrom = Chromosome('Chr1')
chrom.start_x_position = 50
chrom.end_x_position = 350
chrom.start_y_position = 20
chrom.title_size = 14
# Test with label 'Chr2'
dwg2 = Drawing(400, 100)
chrom._draw_label(dwg2, 'Chr2')
label = dwg2.contents[0]
assert label.text == 'Chr2', f"Expected text='Chr2', got {label.text}"
# Test with numeric label
dwg3 = Drawing(400, 100)
chrom._draw_label(dwg3, '123')
label = dwg3.contents[0]
assert label.text == '123', f"Expected text='123', got {label.text}"
# Test with empty string
dwg4 = Drawing(400, 100)
chrom._draw_label(dwg4, '')
label = dwg4.contents[0]
assert label.text == '', f"Expected empty text, got {label.text}"
# Test with spaces
dwg5 = Drawing(400, 100)
chrom._draw_label(dwg5, 'name with spaces')
label = dwg5.contents[0]
assert label.text == 'name with spaces', f"Expected text='name with spaces', got {label.text}"
print('Passed label name robustness test')
result = None

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: The method must use the label_name parameter and not hardcode a specific string.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: The drawn label's text must equal the label_name passed to the method.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: Hardcoded label name causes test to fail for non-matching names.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Verify that the label text matches the provided label_name for various inputs.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include hidden tests with multiple label name variants.
